judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports are substantively equivalent and hit all three expectations with correct, well-grounded detail; Report 1 is marginally more thorough on file/line citations.

report_1.evidence: In Postgres, the owner of a table (and any superuser) bypasses row-level security by default, regardless of `FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY` being set or not — and this migration doesn't even set `FORCE`... the owning role sees every row unconditionally, silently, with no error.

report_2.evidence: row-level security policies are never applied to the table's owner unless the table also has `FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY` set (it does not here — only `ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY`, which exempts the owner by default)... every query the app runs...silently bypasses the RLS policy entirely.

report_1.evidence: Change `DATABASE_URL` used by the running application (`cedarline/backend/app/db.py`) to point at `cedar_app`, not `cedar_owner`... `cedar_owner` should be reserved exclusively for schema/migration operations... two separate connection strings/config values going forward, not one shared `DATABASE_URL`

report_2.evidence: Point the application's `DATABASE_URL` at `cedar_app`, not `cedar_owner`... Structurally, migrations (schema/DDL, run as the owning/admin role) and application runtime access (DML, run as a restricted non-owner role subject to RLS) should be strictly separated

report_1.evidence: The `cedar_app` role is created exclusively by `cedarline/db/bootstrap/create_app_role.sql`, which its own header comment says is "Not part of automatic database initialization; run by hand on first setup" — it is not invoked from `docker-compose.yml`, not from `scripts/migrate.sh`, and not from `scripts/reset-local-db.sh`. So after the volume is wiped, `reset-local-db.sh` recreates the schema (via `migrate.sh`) but never recreates the `cedar_app` role

report_2.evidence: Nowhere in `reset-local-db.sh` is `db/bootstrap/create_app_role.sql` re-run. That script is explicitly documented as a manual, one-time step (`create_app_role.sql:1-2`, and `docs/local-setup.md:6` lists it only under "First time" setup, not under "Reset your local db")
